name: video-bug-analyzer
description: Diagnose UI and rendering bugs from a screen recording — and, more broadly, reason over any clip's look + motion. Give Claude a video and roughly when the bug happens; the plugin extracts frames — an overview contact sheet, scene-cut detection, per-timestamp zoom with before/after strips, or an ROI time-stack (--stack) — then reasons over them to pinpoint the defect. Quantifies FPS stutter / dropped frames and freeze gaps (--stutter) and frame-pacing jitter (--pacing). Doubles as an art/aesthetic-reference tool: --palette pulls a clip's dominant hex ramp, --palette --over-time emits the colour *arc* (t,[hex...] per window) so a loop's colour journey is visible, and --loop-check reports the first-vs-last-frame seam diff (+ a strip) for a seamless loop; GIF input works on every mode. Runs never overwrite a previous extraction, and --check-update compares the installed version against the marketplace. Strong on persistent visual bugs, honest about flicker, timing, and off-screen state. Requires ffmpeg (auto-installs where the sandbox allows).
